name: Eger
summary: Eger is a charming historic city in northern Hungary known for exceptional safety, with very low crime rates and a welcoming atmosphere for tourists. The city offers excellent emergency services, quality healthcare, and efficient transport options for visitors exploring its famous castle, thermal baths, and wine regions.
quickVerdict: Eger stands out as one of Hungary's safest destinations, with minimal violent crime and low petty theft rates. The historic city center, Castle Hill, and wine cellars are all perfectly safe to explore. While standard travel precautions apply, particularly around tourist areas, visitors can feel confident exploring this beautiful destination day or night. The city's compact size, friendly locals, and well-maintained infrastructure make it ideal for solo travelers, families, and couples alike.

neighborhoods.name: Historic City Center
neighborhoods.description: The heart of Eger with the main square, cathedral, and shopping areas. Excellent lighting, regular police presence, and plenty of foot traffic. Very safe for tourists with numerous restaurants and attractions.

neighborhoods.name: Castle Hill District
neighborhoods.description: Area around the famous Eger Castle with historic attractions and museums. Well-maintained tourist zone with excellent security and clear signage. One of the safest areas in the city.

neighborhoods.name: Szépasszonyvölgy (Valley of Beautiful Women)
neighborhoods.description: Famous wine cellar district popular with tourists for wine tasting. Generally safe but can get busy during peak season. Well-monitored area with good lighting along main paths.

neighborhoods.name: Thermal Bath Area
neighborhoods.description: District surrounding the thermal baths and spa facilities. Safe and well-maintained with good facilities for tourists. Regular security presence around bath complexes.

neighborhoods.name: Train Station District
neighborhoods.description: Area around the main railway station, about 1.5km from city center. Generally safe but less monitored than central areas. Some areas may be poorly lit at night but no significant safety concerns.

neighborhoods.name: Residential Outer Districts
neighborhoods.description: Suburban areas including Felnémet and other residential neighborhoods. Very safe with low crime rates but fewer tourist facilities. Quiet areas with minimal foot traffic in evenings.

scams.name: Restaurant Overcharging
scams.description: Some tourist restaurants may add service charges or inflate prices on bills, particularly in wine cellars during peak season.
scams.howToAvoid: Check menus for prices before ordering, ask about service charges, and verify bills before paying. Learn basic Hungarian phrases for ordering.

scams.name: Fake Wine Sales
scams.description: Counterfeit or low-quality wines sold as premium Egri Bikavér (Bull's Blood) by unauthorized sellers, particularly around tourist areas.
scams.howToAvoid: Only buy wine from licensed cellars and established shops. Ask for certificates of authenticity for expensive bottles and avoid street vendors.

scams.name: Transport Ticket Fraud
scams.description: Occasional reports of fake ticket inspectors on public transport or overcharging by unofficial taxi services.
scams.howToAvoid: Buy tickets from official vendors, validate transport tickets properly, and use only licensed taxi services or ride-sharing apps.

soloFemale.overview: Eger is exceptionally safe for solo female travelers with very low crime rates and a respectful local culture. The city's compact size makes it easy to navigate, and locals are generally helpful and welcoming. Evening walks in the city center and main tourist areas are considered safe, with good lighting and regular foot traffic.
tips: Stay in accommodations near the city center or Castle Hill for easy access to attractions and better lighting
The thermal baths have strict etiquette rules - shower before entering and wear appropriate swimwear
Wine tasting tours are generally safe but drink responsibly and stick to established cellars
Emergency services speak English and response times are excellent throughout the city

nightSafety.overview: Eger maintains excellent safety standards after dark with well-lit streets in the city center and tourist areas. The historic center remains active in the evenings with restaurants and cafes, providing natural surveillance. Crime rates are very low at night, though standard precautions are advisable in less central areas.
tips: Stick to well-lit main streets and avoid isolated areas outside the city center
The Castle Hill area is beautifully illuminated and safe for evening visits
Wine cellars in Szépasszonyvölgy have good lighting but arrange transport back to your accommodation
Local police patrol regularly and emergency response is quick throughout the night

transport.metro: No metro system. Local buses connect different districts and are safe and reliable with regular service during daytime hours.
transport.rideshare: Limited ride-sharing options. Traditional taxis are available and generally honest, though agree on fares beforehand for longer distances.
transport.taxis: Licensed taxis are safe and regulated. Use official taxi stands or call established companies. Agree on fares before longer trips to wine regions.
transport.tips: The city is highly walkable with most attractions within walking distance. Train connections to Budapest are excellent and safe. Bicycle rentals are available for exploring surrounding areas.

customs: Remove shoes when entering Hungarian homes and bring flowers or wine as gifts
Don't clink beer glasses when toasting - this custom relates to 1848 revolution history
Tip 10-15% in restaurants and give tips directly to servers in cash
Learn basic Hungarian greetings like 'Jó napot' (good day) - locals appreciate the effort
Respect thermal bath etiquette: shower before entering pools and wear swim caps in lap pools

health.overview: Hungary has a well-developed healthcare system with good medical facilities. Eger has quality hospitals and clinics with emergency services available 24/7.
health.water: Tap water is safe to drink throughout Hungary. Bottled water is widely available if preferred.
health.vaccinations: No special vaccinations required for Hungary. Routine vaccines should be up to date. EU citizens can use European Health Insurance Card.
health.altitude: Eger is at low elevation with a temperate continental climate. No altitude-related health concerns. Thermal baths provide health benefits but follow posted guidelines.

faq.a: Yes, Eger is very safe for tourists with low crime rates and excellent emergency services. The city has a safety index of 63.48, making it one of Hungary's safest destinations. Violent crime is extremely rare and petty theft is uncommon.

Eger is generally safe at night, particularly in the city center and around major attractions. Streets are well-lit and there's regular police presence. Exercise normal caution and stick to main areas after dark.

faq.q: Is Eger safe for solo female travelers?
faq.a: Absolutely. Eger is exceptionally safe for solo female travelers with respectful locals and very low harassment rates. The city's compact size and good infrastructure make it ideal for independent exploration.

faq.q: What areas should I avoid in Eger?
faq.a: Eger has no specific no-go areas. All tourist districts including the city center, Castle Hill, and wine valleys are safe. Use standard caution in less central residential areas at night.

faq.q: Is it safe to use public transport in Eger?
faq.a: Yes, local buses and transport are safe and reliable. The city is very walkable so many visitors prefer walking. Train connections to Budapest and other cities are excellent and secure.
